title: erica herbacea subsp. occidentalis
intro: Erica herbacea subsp. occidentalis, also known as Western Spring Heath, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Western Spring Heath include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Erica herbacea subsp. occidentalis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for topical and internal use, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
